name: code-style-enforcer
description: Analyzes code for style consistency and applies project-specific formatting conventions beyond what linters catch. Use when reviewing code, ensuring style consistency, or when user requests code style improvements.
capabilities: Detect project style configuration files (ESLint, Prettier, RuboCop, etc.)
Analyze existing code patterns to identify project conventions
Find naming, organization, and formatting inconsistencies
Suggest automated tools for enforcing style standards
Generate specific style recommendations with before/after examples
Review code against industry style guides
author: CrazyDubya
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. All static findings are false positives - the flagged patterns are code examples in documentation showing recommended tools (ESLint, Prettier, black, rubocop) and API patterns. The skill only uses Read, Grep, Glob for analysis and provides recommendations without execution.
valueStatement: Codebases often drift from consistent style over time. This skill detects style inconsistencies, identifies project-specific conventions, and provides actionable recommendations to maintain code quality across your codebase.
limitations: Does not modify code files directly
Does not install or configure linting tools
Does not analyze runtime behavior or bugs
Relies on project having existing code to reference

useCases.title: Team Style Standards
useCases.description: Ensure all contributors follow the same conventions across the codebase

useCases.title: Pre-commit Checks
useCases.description: Review code for style issues before merging pull requests

useCases.title: Onboarding Help
useCases.description: Learn project conventions by analyzing existing code patterns

promptTemplates.title: Quick Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the code in this project for style consistency. Check naming conventions, formatting, and organization patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Convention Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the codebase to identify existing style conventions. Look at naming, file structure, and formatting patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Style Fixes
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate specific style recommendations for improving code consistency. Include before and after examples.

promptTemplates.title: Tool Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Recommend linting and formatting tools that match the project's existing style conventions.

bestPractices: Follow existing patterns even when not ideal for consistency across the codebase
Use automated tools like Prettier or Black in CI/CD pipelines to enforce style
Document style decisions in CONTRIBUTING.md for new contributors
antiPatterns: Mixing different naming conventions in the same file or module
Leaving magic numbers without constants that explain their purpose
Inconsistent error handling patterns across similar functions

faq.question: Which languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Ruby, Go, Java, C++, Rust, PHP, Swift, Kotlin and other common languages.

faq.question: What style issues can it detect?
faq.answer: Naming conventions, indentation, line length, import ordering, comment styles, and code organization patterns.

faq.question: Does it integrate with existing linters?
faq.answer: It reads linter configurations and suggests tools but does not install or configure them automatically.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads code for analysis. No code is sent anywhere or modified without user approval.

faq.question: Why use this over standard linters?
faq.answer: Linters enforce syntax rules. This skill catches project-specific conventions, naming patterns, and style issues that linters miss.

faq.question: How is this different from Prettier?
faq.answer: Prettier formats code automatically. This skill analyzes patterns, explains issues, and recommends solutions with context.
